title: micranthes manchuriensis var. octopetala
intro: Micranthes manchuriensis var. octopetala, also known as Manchurian saxifrage, is a plant species within the Saxifragaceae family, characterized by its unique, eight-petaled flowers. It is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities, and is used to treat various health conditions. The plant has been found to have several health benefits, including reducing fever and relieving pain, as well as its potential to help manage digestive issues. In traditional herbal medicine, Micranthes manchuriensis var. octopetala is often prepared as a decoction or infusion, and can also be used in the form of a tincture or dried herbal supplement.
